rules.description: Buttons should typically be translated as verbs using the infinitive form
rules.example_correct: Bestand openen

rules.description: Be concise, avoid using articles or prepositions and go straight to the point
rules.example_correct: Annuleren

rules.description: Translations of buttons do not end in a period, unless they consist of more than one sentence

rules.section: Software Localization - Names of RingCentral Products
rules.description: Product/application names are often trademarked, thus not to be localized
rules.notes: Check reference material and linguistic instructions, including terminology lists before translating

rules.description: Replace English examples by Dutch equivalents when needed
rules.notes: Example given for wildcard operators explanation

rules.description: Use formal addressing of people: u/uw (you/your) for business context
rules.notes: Target audience are businesses

rules.description: Register should be professional and welcoming, business casual
rules.notes: Be precise and clear, consistent and natural

rules.description: Rephrase sentences that are not clear enough, and rework sentences that sound like a translation
rules.notes: Do not hesitate to break up long English sentences into smaller parts

rules.description: Avoid an unnecessarily formal or pompous tone
rules.notes: Refrain from using words that add formality without adding meaning, like 'tevens', 'met ingang van', 'betreffende'

rules.description: Content should be accurate and concise (exact): no ambiguities or mistranslations
Content should be clear and coherent (readable): target reader should understand the text easily
Content should be objective: neutral, do not insert your own opinion, avoid colloquial or slang words, be always politically correct, aim for bias-free language
Content should be correct: content and numbers should be free of mistakes
Content should be complete: do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs)
Avoid a condescending, childish or overly familiar tone and treat the reader as an adult and a professional
rules.notes: Strive for clarity without resorting to unnecessary repetition

rules.description: Downplay humor and keep the tone business-like
Use idiomatically correct language: use of correct verbs, metaphors, expressions, etc. so that text does not sound like a translation
rules.notes: Applies to Marketing Content, E-mails, Product Guides, PR letters, Customer Proposals and selected UI components

rules.description: Avoid literal translations. Balance faithfulness to the source and readability in the target language
Avoid the passive voice and the use of the word 'men'. Use the active form to improve readability
rules.notes: Passive voice is only advised in specific texts like legal and official documents

rules.description: Avoid the use of the second-person possessive 'uw bestanden'. Simple articles can often replace possessive adjectives
Cross-references have to be referenced consistently throughout your translation as well as throughout all files
If the external cross-reference document has not been translated before, leave as in source language
Do not hesitate to be more direct and use the imperative form where appropriate instead of translating 'Please'
rules.example_correct: Voer uw gebruikersnaam in.
rules.example_incorrect: Gelieve uw gebruikersnaam in te voeren.
rules.notes: Dutch does not require the same caution as English

rules.description: Try to avoid gendered sentences. When referring to persons, if possible use words like 'deze' and 'die' instead of 'hij' or 'zij'
rules.example_correct: Het serviceteam neemt contact op met de klant en informeert deze.
rules.example_incorrect: Het serviceteam neemt contact op met de klant en informeert hem/haar.
rules.notes: Find a good balance between bias-free language and fluency

rules.description: Use specific article choices for RingCentral translations
rules.example_correct: het touchscreen, het account, de display, de webinar, de whitepaper, de badge
rules.notes: Contact RingCentral Dutch language lead when encountering other nouns that can have both de/het

rules.description: Avoid bloated expressions like 'die het mogelijk maken...'
rules.notes: Applies to most product UI, Support Content and Customer Training Materials

rules.description: Avoid overly long or complex compounds. Unintuitive compounds are an intelligibility and usability issue
'Please' and 'sorry' are not translated
rules.notes: Applies to Marketing Content, E-mails, Product Guides, PR letters, Customer Proposals and selected UI components

rules.description: 'Successfully' should not be included in the Dutch translation
Avoid starting a sentence with 'Om...'
Avoid starting a sentence with 'Bijvoorbeeld'
Avoid overtranslation. Use 'met' instead of 'met behulp van' where equally clear
rules.notes: Overtranslation is the use of unnecessary words where a less verbose version would be equally clear

rules.description: Avoid literal translations that ignore Dutch rules for style and idiom
rules.example_correct: Complexe formules zijn ook eenvoudiger samen te stellen als ze bestaan uit kleinere formules die een naam hebben.
rules.example_incorrect: U kunt het ook gemakkelijker vinden om complexe formules te schrijven als onderdelen van die formules benoemde, korte formules bevatten.
rules.notes: Literal translations can result in sentences that are hard to understand

rules.description: Restructure sentences for better Dutch flow and understanding
rules.example_correct: Klik op de knop Volgende als u het product hebt geselecteerd.
rules.example_incorrect: Wanneer u het product geselecteerd hebt, klik op de knop Volgende.

rules.description: Use natural Dutch expressions rather than literal translations
rules.example_correct: Omdat de GPS de batterij snel kan ontladen, kunt u deze maar een beperkte tijd gebruiken.
rules.example_incorrect: Omdat de GPS de batterij snel kan ontladen, wilt u deze slechts een beperkte tijd gebruiken.

rules.description: The RingCentral Glossary is your first and binding source for technical terms
For terms not listed in the glossary stick to Microsoft standard terminology where applicable
rules.notes: Reference: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/language

rules.description: Use available online resources like Wikipedia, administrator web pages, large IT vendor portals etc. for terminology
rules.notes: In case of doubt check back with your PM or Language Lead

rules.description: When referring to UI elements, always use Uppercase
The first word following a colon should not be written with a capital in Dutch, unless there is more than one sentence after the colon
rules.notes: If more than one sentence follows the colon, the first word should be written with a capital

rules.description: In sentences and headings, only use uppercase at the beginning of the sentence
Follow Dutch rules for titles and headings even if fully capitalized in English
rules.notes: Exception: legal texts where capitalization conveys specific meaning and must be retained

rules.description: Product names follow the source capitalization
Use accents on uppercase letters to avoid pronunciation problems
rules.example_correct: GEÏNTEGREERD, GEÜPLOAD, BELGIË

rules.description: Use lowercase in months, days and currencies
Run the spell-checker when you have completed your translation
rules.notes: Copy the translated word into Word to use the grammar checker too

rules.description: Brand name tags need to be separated from the preceding or following text with a space
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces in Dutch
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces after full stops

rules.description: Use non-breaking spaces to prevent certain expressions to be separated at the end of a line
rules.notes: For people's names, numbers, software names and versions, etc. Use Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or Alt+0160

rules.description: Do not use non-breaking spaces in software files
Use a comma in situations where a sentence would otherwise be too long or complicated, or where omission could cause confusion
rules.notes: Do not use the comma to separate two sentences

rules.description: Acronyms and units of measurement are not spelled with periods
The period is used as a separator in thousands (but not in years, postal codes, phone numbers)
The period is used as a separator in Dutch bank account numbers (Not Belgian) and version numbers
Do not use full stops after headings and titles, after columns in a table, or after captions used with illustrations, tables etc., unless the expression is a full sentence
Do not use full stops if a sentence ends with an abbreviation, an interrogation or exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon
Try to avoid using colons, especially in marketing related content
Use colons to introduce lists and at the end of a sentence that introduces a procedure
The first word after the colon does not get a capital, if the item following the colon is a single sentence
Semicolons are preferably avoided. After a semicolon a complete sentence must follow, with a personal form
For marketing and PR content semicolons should never be used. Cut the sentence in two smaller ones to enhance readability
In front and behind an ellipsis in general there is a space. However, if a word is aborted, there is no space in front
rules.example_correct: Installeren van software ...

rules.description: When ellipsis dots are used in English, they must be used in Dutch as well
Use normal hyphen (-) to connect the components of certain compounds
rules.example_correct: PowerPoint-presentatie
rules.notes: Consult the Taalunie rules regarding the use of hyphens in compounds

rules.description: Use hyphen to separate words across a line break and as ellipsis marker for omitted components
rules.example_correct: hard- en software, pagina's 15-18
rules.notes: The ellipted component should be a word part of equal value, not a separate word

rules.description: The hyphen is also used as the minus sign
Do not use the En or Em-dash. Replace En or Em dashes that appear in English text by commas or brackets
rules.example_correct: De aard van de gegevens in de cel (tekst, een getal of een formule) heeft invloed op de uitlijning ervan.
rules.example_incorrect: De aard van de gegevens in de cel – tekst, een getal of een formule – heeft invloed op de uitlijning ervan

rules.description: Use round brackets for further explications which would otherwise disrupt the sentence
rules.example_correct: Vul de gevraagde gegevens (getallen) op de stippellijn in.
rules.notes: The first letter is a lower case unless the sentence in parentheses is an independent sentence

rules.description: Use round brackets when adding the full form of an acronym
rules.example_correct: XML (Extended Markup Language)

rules.description: The area code of phone numbers is written between brackets
Use square brackets for indicating variables or for indicating additional information within round brackets
rules.example_correct: Geef het pad op (UNC-pad [intern] of URL [extern]) en klik vervolgens op Opslaan.

rules.description: Only single straight quotation marks are used to mark names of chapters, sections and appendixes of manuals
rules.example_correct: Wilt u de wijzigingen in 'Profiel' opslaan?
rules.notes: Don't use single quotes around placeholders even if the placeholders are enclosed with quotes in English source

rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables
Double quotes are only used for direct quotations
rules.example_correct: Het kantoor was zogenaamd "tijdelijk" gesloten.

rules.description: In English, file names and other variables are often placed between single quotation marks. In Dutch, we omit these
Use straight apostrophes consistently in the translations, instead of curly apostrophes
Do not use abbreviations like 'zo'n' or ''t'
Use the same format in your translation as in the original text for enumerations
rules.notes: Items are usually preceded by a bullet or a sequential number

rules.description: The sentence introducing the enumeration ends with a colon. Each part starts with a capital letter and ends with a period
rules.example_correct:
De volgende gevallen zijn mogelijk:
● De server bevindt zich in het netwerk.
● De server bevindt zich buiten het netwerk.

rules.description: If the parts of an enumeration consist of a single word or a small group of words, the punctuation marks may be omitted altogether
rules.example_correct:
De server kan zich op de volgende locaties bevinden:
● binnen het netwerk
● buiten het netwerk

rules.description: Each part of the enumeration starts with a lowercase letter and ends with a semicolon. Only the last part gets a period
Do not use abbreviations in body text. Write expressions in full
rules.example_correct: met betrekking tot, ten aanzien van

rules.description: Abbreviations are only used when absolutely necessary, except for units or when there are space issues
rules.notes: Be consistent and do not abbreviate a term in some sentences and spell it out in other sentences

rules.description: If there is insufficient space, articles and prepositions can often be omitted first, without losing content or clarity
rules.notes: However, do not use this 'telegram style' if it is not necessary

rules.description: Write initiaalwoorden and letterwoorden without any periods
rules.example_correct: pincode, cd-romstation, btw-tarief, pc-gebruiker, AOW'er, tv-kijker, RAM-geheugen
rules.notes: Follow the capitalization of the abbreviated words

rules.description: For letterwoorden with capitals and initiaalwoorden, retain the capital and apostrophe in compounds
rules.example_correct: AOW'er

rules.description: When an acronym makes its first appearance, spell it out. Put the full term between brackets, immediately after the acronym
rules.example_correct: OLE (objecten koppelen en insluiten)
rules.notes: After this initial clarification, the acronym can be used without the full term

rules.description: If the acronym refers to a term not normally translated into Dutch, give only the full English term
Certain acronyms require no explanation
rules.example_correct: ADSL MS-DOS GUI pc ASCII ANSI EGA VGA PCI ROM UNIX TCP/IP cd-rom dvd-rom

rules.description: Very few acronyms are localized in Dutch. Most are names or organizations or countries
rules.example_correct: VK, VSA, VAE, EU, VN

rules.description: Numbers should be written in full when used as nouns
Numbers should be written in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities, usually up to 12
Numbers should be written in full in expressions like 'in the 80s'
Dates, large numbers, ages, document references should be written as numbers
Replace the decimal point by a comma and the thousand separator by a dot
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
By law, the Netherlands uses the metric system. Avoid pounds, miles, inches, feet and gallons
rules.notes: These have no legal meaning

rules.description: Units of measure should be separated from the preceding number by a non-breaking space
After abbreviations of units of measurement there is no period
rules.notes: According to official Dutch standards

rules.description: Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches and represent one of the few exceptions
The percent sign is %, without a leading space
Default Short Date Format: d-m-yy
rules.notes: Month without leading zero, day without leading zero, year without century

rules.description: Default Long Date Format: dddd d mmmm yyyy
rules.example_correct: dinsdag 26 mei 2020 OR 26 mei 2020
rules.notes: Names of days are optional, month name spelled out completely, names of workdays and months start with lower case

rules.description: The names of the months and days start with a lower case
rules.example_correct: januari, februari, maart, maandag, dinsdag

rules.description: Use specified abbreviations for months and days
rules.example_correct: jan, feb, maa, apr, mei, jun, jul, aug, sep, okt, nov, dec; ma, di, wo, do, vr, za, zo
rules.notes: Note that 'mei' has no abbreviation

rules.description: In a general context, the fully written form uur is preferred. Uur can be abbreviated to u.
rules.example_correct: De vergadering begint om 8.30 uur.
rules.notes: Uur or u. are written after the numbers, not between them

rules.description: In technical and scientific texts the symbols h, min and s are common
rules.example_correct: 17 h 03 min 16 s
rules.notes: These symbols are written between the figures

rules.description: The symbol h is also common for speed indications, next to u (without a period)
rules.example_correct: Hij reed met een snelheid van 150 km/h.

rules.description: Currency amounts can be written in three ways with the Euro sign followed by a space being preferred
rules.notes: The first variant with the Euro sign is preferred

rules.description: Use established translations for common expressions
rules.example_correct: Over ... (About XYZ); zoals weergegeven in tabel xx (as shown in table xx); Voor meer informatie over xxx, kunt u xxx raadplegen (For more information on xxx, see xxx)

rules.description: Use specific translations for common phrases and instructions
rules.example_correct: Houd er rekening mee dat... (Please note that); Volg deze stappen om xxx (Follow these steps to xxx); Weet u zeker dat u… wilt...? (Are you sure you want to...?)

rules.description: Pay attention to the correct use of prepositions in translations
rules.example_correct: migreren naar (migrate to); importeren uit/in (import from/to); exporteren naar (export to); integratie met (integration with)
rules.notes: Consult the Microsoft Language Portal for use of prepositions for Dutch UI content

rules.description: Use correct prepositions for common UI actions
rules.example_correct: klikken (click on); verbinden met (connect to); in de werkbalk (in the toolbar); op internet (on the Web); in het menu (on the menu); op een pagina (on a page)

rules.description: Application/product names are often trademarked and are therefore rarely translated
rules.notes: Before translating any application, product, or feature name, verify that it is translatable and not protected

rules.description: Use trademark and registered trademark symbols only on the first mention in the translated version
rules.notes: Unless otherwise indicated

rules.description: For third party products, research to determine whether an official, approved trademarked translated version is available
rules.notes: Otherwise, use as in source

rules.description: Use hyphens in compounds with brand names to improve readability in Dutch RingCentral content
rules.notes: Even though Groene boekje suggests concatenation without hyphens

rules.description: Use the specified Dutch translation for copyright notices when no official approved version is available
rules.example_correct: ©2015 RingCentral, Inc. Alle rechten voorbehouden. RingCentral, RingCentral Office, RingCentral Meetings en het RingCentral-logo zijn geregistreerde handelsmerken van RingCentral, Inc.

rules.description: Query if URLs have a local version and modify the document accordingly
rules.notes: If not, leave the relevant URL as it appears in the source English text

rules.description: If software has been translated, match the relevant translation memory or string list provided
rules.example_correct: Klik op Bestand openen.

rules.description: If software has not been translated, use bilingual format: English first, followed by target in brackets
rules.example_correct: Klik op Open File (Bestand openen).
rules.notes: Put the translation outside the formatting

rules.description: When Microsoft terminology or Microsoft UI items are quoted, refer to Microsoft approved terminology
rules.notes: Reference: http://www.microsoft.com/Language/en-US/Default.aspx

rules.description: Translate country and city names based on your country/region's conventions
Localize address formats based on your country/region's conventions
Add an international country code to local numbers for international users when source only includes toll free and US numbers
